words.meaning: involving the chance of loss or danger
words.example: It is risky to share passwords with friends.

words.meaning: full of too many people or things
words.example: The crowded train made the trip uncomfortable.
A nearby clinic offers free health checks.

words.meaning: existing or happening around the world
words.example: The product became a worldwide success.

words.meaning: existing or happening across a whole country
words.example: The company started a nationwide recycling campaign.
The speech was emotional because it described real loss.
The final project is optional for advanced students.

words.meaning: required by a rule or law
words.example: Compulsory education lasts for several years.

words.meaning: using physical signals rather than digital data
words.example: An analog clock can still be useful in classrooms.

words.meaning: related to soldiers or armed forces
words.example: Military service is required in some countries.
